page.primary_keyword: marbella yacht charter f1 grand prix 2026
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter F1 Grand Prix 2026: Watch the Race from the Sea

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix 2026. Watch the race from the sea in Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h. Enquire now.
data.summary: This page explains how to experience the F1 Grand Prix 2026 from a private yacht in Marbella, with fleet options, pricing, and key tips for booking. It covers departure ports, inclusions, and why watching from the water beats the crowds.
key_facts: F1 Grand Prix 2026 dates TBC – typically late May/early June.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, 5 NM from Marbella Marina.
Every charter includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Free jet ski included on Mangusta 80 bookings (4h minimum).
Maximum 12 pax per yacht – book early for F1 weekend.
Watch the race on board with live TV or stream via onboard Wi-Fi.
Combine with sunset cruise or lunch at Cala del Faro.

faq.q: When is the F1 Grand Prix 2026 in Marbella?
faq.a: The exact dates for the 2026 F1 Grand Prix are not yet confirmed, but it typically takes place in late May or early June. The race is held at the Circuito de Jerez-Ángel Nieto, about 90 minutes from Marbella. We recommend booking your yacht charter at least 2–3 months in advance for the best availability.

faq.q: Can I watch the F1 Grand Prix from a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, you can watch the race live on board via satellite TV or stream it on the yacht's Wi-Fi. Many guests prefer to anchor near Puerto Banús and enjoy the race with drinks and snacks. The atmosphere is electric, and you avoid the crowded race stands.

faq.q: Which yachts are available for the F1 Grand Prix 2026?
faq.a: We offer a range of yachts from 6.4m to 29m. Popular choices include the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (€749/2h, 11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, 12 pax, with free jet ski). For larger groups, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) is available on request.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees. You just need to bring your own swimwear and sun cream.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the F1 Grand Prix 2026?
faq.a: You can contact us directly via WhatsApp or email. Provide your preferred date, number of guests, and yacht type. We will confirm availability and send a booking link. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due 7 days before departure.

faq.q: What time should we depart for the F1 Grand Prix?
faq.a: The race usually starts in the afternoon (around 3 PM local time). We recommend departing at least 2 hours before the race to anchor in a good spot. You can also combine a morning cruise with a stop for lunch at Cala del Faro or Cabopino.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and decorations. Many guests bring F1-themed banners or flags. The yacht has a fridge and a small galley. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the space. Our standard drinks package is included.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on race day?
faq.a: We monitor weather forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe (high winds or rough seas), we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so bad weather is rare in late May.

data.body_html:
The F1 Grand Prix is one of the most exhilarating events of the year, and watching it from the deck of a private yacht in Marbella takes the experience to another level. Instead of fighting crowds and queuing for drinks, you can anchor in the calm waters of Puerto Banús, tune into the race on a large screen, and enjoy unlimited drinks and snacks with your friends. With our Marbella yacht charter fleet, you can book a boat for as little as €749 for two hours or splurge on a 24-metre Mangusta 80 with a free jet ski. Here is everything you need to know to plan your F1 weekend at sea.

Why Watch the F1 Grand Prix from a Yacht in Marbella?

Marbella is the perfect base for an F1 weekend because you get the race-day thrill combined with the Costa del Sol lifestyle. The Circuito de Jerez is only a 90-minute drive away, but many fans prefer to stay on the coast and watch the race from a boat. The atmosphere in Puerto Banús is electric: supercars, designer boutiques, and a buzzing marina. On a yacht, you have front-row seats to the race broadcast, plus the freedom to swim, sunbathe, and enjoy a barbecue between laps. It is a far more relaxed and luxurious way to experience Grand Prix weekend.

Our Fleet for the F1 Grand Prix 2026

We operate a diverse fleet of yachts, all departing from Puerto Banús. For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are the most affordable options at €749 for two hours or €2,299 for a full day (8 hours). For a premium experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, available in grey or white) costs €4,719 for a minimum of four hours and includes a free jet ski. Larger groups can request the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) – pricing on request via WhatsApp. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.

Best Yachts for Groups of 10–12

If you are planning a group outing for the F1 Grand Prix, here are the best options for 10 to 12 guests:

  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – spacious flybridge for viewing the race.
  • Mangusta 80 (White or Grey) (24m, 12 pax) – includes free jet ski, ideal for adrenaline lovers.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – the ultimate luxury, with multiple decks and a jacuzzi.
  • K80 (24m, 12 pax) – modern design with large sun pads and a shaded cockpit.

We recommend booking at least three months in advance for the F1 weekend, as these yachts sell out quickly.

What to Expect on the Day

Your charter begins at Puerto Banús. You will meet your skipper at the dock, board the yacht, and receive a safety briefing. You can bring your own F1-themed decorations, flags, or even a portable speaker for music. The yacht will have a TV or projector, and we can help you connect to the live race stream. During the race, the skipper will anchor in a calm spot near the marina. After the race, you can cruise along the coast to Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a swim, or head back to Puerto Banús for dinner at a beach club.

Pricing and Booking Details

YachtLengthMax PaxPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€749€2,299
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719 (4h min)N/A
Other yachtsVariesUp to 12Pricing on request

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. For the Mangusta 80, the price is for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. To book, contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date and group size.

Tips for the Best Experience

  • Book early – F1 weekend is high season, and our fleet books up fast.
  • Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat – the sun is strong in May/June.
  • Consider a full-day charter (8h) to combine the race with a coastal cruise to Estepona or Sotogrande.
  • If you choose the Mangusta 80, use the jet ski between qualifying and the race.
  • Check the weather forecast – we will notify you if conditions are unsafe.

Frequently asked questions

When is the F1 Grand Prix 2026 in Marbella?

The exact dates for the 2026 F1 Grand Prix are not yet confirmed, but it typically takes place in late May or early June. The race is held at the Circuito de Jerez-Ángel Nieto, about 90 minutes from Marbella. We recommend booking your yacht charter at least 2–3 months in advance for the best availability.

Can I watch the F1 Grand Prix from a yacht in Marbella?

Yes, you can watch the race live on board via satellite TV or stream it on the yacht's Wi-Fi. Many guests prefer to anchor near Puerto Banús and enjoy the race with drinks and snacks. The atmosphere is electric, and you avoid the crowded race stands.

Which yachts are available for the F1 Grand Prix 2026?

We offer a range of yachts from 6.4m to 29m. Popular choices include the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (€749/2h, 11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, 12 pax, with free jet ski). For larger groups, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) is available on request.

What is included in the charter price?

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees. You just need to bring your own swimwear and sun cream.

How do I book a yacht for the F1 Grand Prix 2026?

You can contact us directly via WhatsApp or email. Provide your preferred date, number of guests, and yacht type. We will confirm availability and send a booking link. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due 7 days before departure.

What time should we depart for the F1 Grand Prix?

The race usually starts in the afternoon (around 3 PM local time). We recommend departing at least 2 hours before the race to anchor in a good spot. You can also combine a morning cruise with a stop for lunch at Cala del Faro or Cabopino.

Can we bring our own food and decorations?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and decorations. Many guests bring F1-themed banners or flags. The yacht has a fridge and a small galley. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the space. Our standard drinks package is included.

What happens if the weather is bad on race day?

We monitor weather forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe (high winds or rough seas), we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so bad weather is rare in late May.
